title: PCPipe: Protein clustering with SIMAP annotations
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7cle8","text":"PCPipe is a protein-clustering tool.  The input is a set of ORFs and a FASTA file with already clustered ORFs. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f57r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6rjud","text":"The process entails:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"csnsq","text":"Use cd-hit-2d to compare the input peptides to previously clustered proteins","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":4,"length":9}],"data":[]},{"key":"3nqt","text":"The result is a file with input proteins that clustered to existing clusters and those that did not","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b2g7n","text":"Use the unclustered peptides and self-cluster them via cd-hit","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":55,"length":6}],"data":[]},{"key":"c4mnp","text":"Take a representative sequence from each novel cluster, and use \"blastp\" to compare to SIMAP.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":65,"length":6},{"key":3,"offset":87,"length":5}],"data":[]},{"key":"2n14f","text":"Use the resulting SIMAP \"feature_id\" to look up the SIMAP features, merging the query results with the protein ID into a tab-delimited annotations file","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":4,"offset":52,"length":14}],"data":[]},{"key":"8lm93","text":"Provide the user with two cluster files and the annotations for the new clusters based on the representative sequence","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ck12b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"kq83","text":"Code steps: Login to the iPlant/CyVerse " Discovery Environment ." Choose the "Apps" button on the left, then navigate to "Public Apps -> Experimental -> iMicrobe -> PCPipe." Click on the "PCPipe" app or highlight it and choose "Apps -> Use App..." from the "Apps" menu bar.
Upload your data into the Data Store.

Indicate the directory containing the proteins/ORFs you wish to cluster along with the existing cluster file. Both files should be in FASTA format. You can alter the minimum number of members in a cluster from the default of '2' to make the clustering more stringent. Press "Launch Analysis" and wait for an email saying that the job has completed.

See the "pcpipe-out" directory for the following: "cdhit-2d-outdir" directory containing the clustered proteins and the "novel.fa" unclustered proteins "cdhit-outdir" directory containing self-clustered proteins A file called "novel.fa" of the representative sequences from the self-clustered proteins A file called "blast.out" containing the "blastp" results of the "novel.fa" proteins to SIMAP A file called "simap-annotations.tab" showing the protein ID merged with SIMAP feature data a
creator: Ken Youens-Clark
